name: Threads

x.coordination: OS-scheduled kernel threads share one address space; they coordinate through shared memory plus synchronisation primitives (mutexes, atomics, condition variables).
hazards: Data races on unsynchronised shared mutable state
Deadlock / livelock from inconsistent lock ordering

def: Independently-scheduled execution flows within a process sharing its address space.
problem: Run work concurrently and exploit multiple cores within one process.
mechanism: The OS schedules kernel threads onto cores; threads share memory, requiring synchronisation for shared mutable state.
limits: Shared mutable state → data races, deadlocks
failures: Data races / torn reads without synchronisation
whenNot: Tasks needing millions of units (use lightweight tasks)
myths: "More threads = faster" — oversubscription thrashes the scheduler
